facts: In 1988, the Morris Worm infected ~6,000 computers — about 10% of the entire internet at the time.
Kevin Mitnick, once the world's most wanted hacker, later became a cybersecurity consultant after serving 5 years in prison.
The first computer virus, 'Creeper', was created in 1971 and displayed the message: 'I'm the creeper, catch me if you can!'
In 1994, Vladimir Levin hacked Citibank and transferred $10.7 million — one of the first major online bank heists.
The word 'hacker' originally meant a skilled programmer — it had no negative meaning until the 1980s media changed it.
In 1983, a 17-year-old hacked into NORAD (US nuclear defense) — this incident inspired the movie WarGames.
The first ATM hack happened in 1993 when hackers discovered ATMs could be put into 'diagnostic mode' by pressing certain keys.
Anonymous, the hacktivist group, took down PayPal, Visa and MasterCard in 2010 during 'Operation Payback'.
In 2010, Stuxnet became the first known cyberweapon — it destroyed Iranian nuclear centrifuges without firing a single bullet.
Gary McKinnon hacked 97 US military and NASA computers in 2001-2002 searching for evidence of UFOs.
The most common password in the world is still '123456' — used by over 23 million accounts.
'password' is the second most common password globally. People really are that predictable.
A GPU can crack an 8-character password using brute force in under 2 hours using modern hardware.
The average person reuses the same password across 14 different websites.
Two-factor authentication blocks 99.9% of automated account takeover attacks.
SQL Injection was first publicly discussed in 1998 — and it is still the #1 web vulnerability 25+ years later.
The Heartbleed bug (2014) affected an estimated 17% of all SSL servers on the internet — about 500,000 websites.
EternalBlue, the NSA exploit leaked in 2017, was later used by WannaCry ransomware to infect 200,000+ systems.
Log4Shell (2021) was called 'the worst vulnerability in a decade' — it affected billions of devices running Java.
The Mirai botnet (2016) infected 600,000 IoT devices — mainly cameras and routers — and took down major websites.
Google has paid out over $50 million in bug bounty rewards since its program launched in 2010.
A 10-year-old Finnish boy earned a $10,000 bug bounty from Instagram for finding a vulnerability in 2016.
HackerOne's platform has paid over $300 million in bug bounties to ethical hackers worldwide.
The most expensive bug bounty ever paid was $2 million — awarded by Zerodium for an iOS exploit chain.
Bug bounty hunters can legally earn more than most software engineers by finding security flaws.
There are over 2,200 cyberattacks per day globally — that is one attack every 39 seconds.
Cybercrime is expected to cost the world $10.5 trillion annually by 2025.
It takes an average of 207 days for a company to identify a data breach.
95% of cybersecurity breaches are caused by human error — not technical failure.
The global cybersecurity workforce shortage is estimated at 3.5 million unfilled jobs.
Nmap (Network Mapper) was released in 1997 and is still the most widely used network scanner today.
Wireshark can capture and analyze over 2,000 different network protocols.
Metasploit Framework contains over 2,000 exploits and is used by both attackers and defenders.
The Tor network, used for anonymous browsing, was originally developed by the US Navy.
Kali Linux contains over 600 penetration testing tools pre-installed out of the box.
WannaCry (2017) infected 230,000+ computers in 150 countries in just 24 hours.
The first ransomware, 'AIDS Trojan', was distributed via floppy disk in 1989.
Colonial Pipeline paid $4.4 million in Bitcoin ransom in 2021 — the US DOJ later recovered $2.3 million of it.
CryptoLocker (2013) was the first modern ransomware — it extorted over $3 million before being shut down.
Ransomware attacks increased by 150% in 2020 — largely due to the COVID-19 work-from-home shift.
The Caesar cipher, used by Julius Caesar in 58 BC, is one of the oldest known encryption methods.
RSA encryption (1977) is still used to secure most of the internet's HTTPS connections today.
SHA-256 — the algorithm behind Bitcoin — would take longer than the age of the universe to crack by brute force.
The Enigma machine used by Nazi Germany had 158 quintillion possible configurations — yet it was broken.
One-time pads are the only mathematically proven unbreakable encryption method — if used correctly.
DEF CON, the world's largest hacking conference, has been held annually in Las Vegas since 1993.
CTF (Capture The Flag) competitions have been used to train cybersecurity professionals since the late 1990s.
The first DEFCON CTF was won by a team called 'Ghetto Hackers' in 1996.
Pwn2Own is a hacking competition where researchers win the devices they successfully exploit — including cars.
OSCP (Offensive Security Certified Professional) is considered one of the hardest and most respected hacking certifications.
